purpose: document.all: IE-only proprietary DOM collection, used as a browser sniff flag and for direct element style access.
modernReplacement: document.getElementById() / document.querySelector(), combined with feature detection.

purpose: document.layers: Netscape 4 proprietary API for CSS-positioned layer objects.
modernReplacement: Standard DOM: document.getElementById() with CSS positioning.

purpose: navigator.userAgent sniffing: Parsing the UA string to branch CSS or feature behavior per browser.
modernReplacement: Feature detection: CSS @supports, window.CSS.supports(), or Modernizr.

purpose: navigator.appName: Legacy browser name check used to branch between IE and Netscape codepaths.
modernReplacement: Feature detection instead of browser name checks.

purpose: currentStyle: IE-only property for reading an element's computed styles.
runtimeStyle: IE-only override style layer that sits above author styles at runtime.
modernReplacement: Direct element.style assignment or CSS custom properties.

purpose: stylesheet.rules: IE-only property on CSSStyleSheet; the standard equivalent is .cssRules.
addRule(): IE-only method to append a CSS rule to a stylesheet.
removeRule(): IE-only method to delete a CSS rule from a stylesheet by index.
document.createStyleSheet(): IE proprietary method to programmatically create a new stylesheet.
modernReplacement: Create and append a <style> element: document.createElement('style'), then append to document.head.

purpose: IE filter via JS: The .filters collection and .filter style assignment applied DirectX visual transforms (opacity, blur, gradients).
modernReplacement: CSS opacity, filter, or backdrop-filter properties.

purpose: Pixel style properties (.pixelLeft, .pixelTop etc.): IE-only numeric pixel accessors on element.style.
